page: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local NVMe storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most multi-core and compute-intensive workloads. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48574.31 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11649.0. Memory bandwidth tests indicate 1894.03 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 163.55 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core (958.0) and single-threaded gzip compression (20.33 GB/sec) falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers.

Given the dedicated ARMv8 core architecture and 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is structured for multi-threaded, scale-out workloads rather than single-threaded speed. It is suited for tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. The custom ARM architecture offers a cost-efficient alternative to x86-based instances for workloads optimized for multi-core scaling, though applications requiring high single-thread performance or x86 instruction sets are not suitable.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARM Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core) and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, with no local storage. Benchmark results place its multi-core compute and memory bandwidth in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48574.31 and a static web serving throughput of 33.12 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is a key tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core scores falling in the bottom 25%. This instance is designed for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations, offering qualitative cost efficiency for scale-out ARM-native applications.

tagline: A dedicated 32-core ARM64 compute instance designed for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and scale-out web serving.

Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Delivers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core metrics
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
